hourlyPerformanceBoxPlot.title: Performance by Hour (Distribution)
hourlyPerformanceBoxPlot.label: PnL Distribution
hourlyPerformanceBoxPlot.boxDescription: Box: Central 50% of trades (Q1 to Q3)
hourlyPerformanceBoxPlot.outliersDescription: Dots: Exceptional values (blue = gains, pink = losses)
hourlyPerformanceBoxPlot.outliersClippedDescription: Triangles at axis edge: off-scale values (see tooltip)

correlation.title: PnL vs Number of Trades Correlation
correlation.label: Correlation
correlation.rSquaredTooltip: The coefficient of determination (R²) measures the strength of the correlation between the number of trades and PnL. The heatmap shows how many trading days fall in each zone (brighter = more days). The regression line shows the general trend.

durationPerformance.title: Performance by trade duration
durationPerformance.tooltip: Average P&L and win rate per duration bucket (same intervals as the dashboard duration distribution). Quickly see which holding times are most profitable and most consistent.

sizePerformance.title: Performance by position size
sizePerformance.tooltip: Average P&L and win rate per position size. Quickly see which sizes are most profitable and most consistent.

featureCorrelationMatrix.title: Feature Correlation Matrix
featureCorrelationMatrix.tooltip: Measures linear correlation between key variables (P/L, size, duration, entry hour, direction). Values close to ±1 indicate strong relationships.
featureCorrelationMatrix.description: Coefficient values range from -1 (strong inverse relationship) to +1 (strong direct relationship). Values near 0 indicate little or no linear correlation.

drawdown.title: Daily Drawdown
drawdown.label: Drawdown
drawdown.tooltip: This chart shows the distance from your best level (peak). The line at 0 represents your peak. The higher the value, the further you are from your best level. When you reach a new peak, the drawdown returns to 0. Percentages (%) represent the percentage of loss from the peak. For example, 10% means you have lost 10% compared to your best level.

heatmap.title: Performance by Day of Week and Hour
heatmap.tooltip: This heatmap shows your performance (PnL) by day of the week and by hour. Each cell represents the total PnL for a day/hour combination. Blue colors indicate gains, pink colors indicate losses. The more intense the color, the higher the value. Only hours with data are displayed, with one hour of context before and after.

pnlDistribution.title: PnL Distribution
pnlDistribution.label: Percentage of trades
pnlDistribution.tooltip: This chart shows the distribution of your PnL (profits and losses) by grouping trades into value intervals. Each bar represents a PnL interval and its height indicates the percentage of trades that fall within that interval. This helps identify the most frequent PnL ranges in your trading.

gainsVsLosses.title: Gains vs Losses Distribution
gainsVsLosses.tooltip: This chart compares the distribution of your gains and losses separately. The left histogram shows the distribution of winning trades by value ranges, and the right one shows the distribution of losing trades. This helps identify patterns and compare the distribution of gains and losses to optimize your strategy.

maeMfe.title: Maximum Adverse Excursion vs Maximum Favorable Excursion
maeMfe.description: MAE shows the worst move against you during a trade; MFE shows the best move in your favor. Bubbles group similar trades.
maeMfe.tooltip: Analysis of Maximum Adverse Excursion (worst move against you) vs Maximum Favorable Excursion (best move in your favor). Points in the upper left indicate well-managed trades (low MAE, high MFE).
maeMfe.noData: No MAE/MFE data available for this period
maeMfe.densityHint: Bubbles are aggregated by MAE/MFE zone. Their size reflects the number of trades and their transparency highlights dense regions.

monthlyPerformance.title: Monthly/Annual Performance
monthlyPerformance.tooltip: This chart shows your performance (PnL) by month. Each bar represents the total PnL for a given month. This helps identify the most profitable months and seasonal trends.

tradingVolume.title: Trading Volume Over Time
tradingVolume.label: Number of trades
tradingVolume.tooltip: This chart shows the number of trades per day over time. This helps identify periods of intense activity and quiet periods.

tradesDistribution.title: Trades Distribution by Result
tradesDistribution.tooltip: This chart shows the distribution of your trades between winners, losers and neutral. This allows you to quickly visualize the ratio of winning vs losing trades.

riskReward.title: Risk/Reward Ratio Over Time
riskReward.label: Average R:R ratio
riskReward.tooltip: This chart shows the evolution of the average risk/reward ratio (R:R) over time. A ratio greater than 1 indicates that average gains are greater than average losses. This helps evaluate the effectiveness of your risk management over time.

radar.title: Overall Performance
radar.subtitle: Key metrics analysis
radar.tooltip: This radar chart displays 5 essential metrics normalized from 0 to 100. The larger the surface, the better your performance. 50 = minimum target, 75+ = excellent performance.
radar.scoreTooltip: Global score calculated as the average of the 5 normalized metrics. Each metric is evaluated on a scale of 0 to 100 based on realistic trading benchmarks. The higher the score, the better your overall performance.

equityCurve.title: Equity Curve
equityCurve.tooltip: This chart shows the evolution of your capital over time. The green curve represents your current capital (initial capital + cumulative PnL). The gray dashed line indicates your initial capital for reference. This allows you to visualize the growth or decline of your capital over time.

monteCarlo.title: Balance target projection
monteCarlo.tooltip: Monte Carlo simulation based on your average daily gain (μ) and daily volatility (σ) from the account's full trading history. Optionally adjust lots and $/point to project a target size (μ and σ scaled linearly vs historical median size×$/point). Assumes stable edge; mixed contracts = approximation.
monteCarlo.noAccount: Select a trading account to run a projection.
monteCarlo.percentileExplainer: Fast = time reached by the 25% most favorable simulations. Prudent = time by which 75% of simulations reach the target (the remaining 25% take longer). Typical = median time (50/50).

cards.medianHint: Median time — half faster, half slower
cards.medianTooltip: Across 10,000 simulations, 50% reach the target in fewer days than this, 50% take longer. This is the central scenario, neither optimistic nor cautious.
cards.optimisticTooltip: Only 25% of simulations reach the target in this time or less — a favorable path, not a guarantee.
cards.prudentHint: 75% of simulations reach the target by then
cards.prudentTooltip: 75% of simulations reach the target in this time or less; 25% take longer. Useful for planning without being too optimistic.
cards.probabilityTooltip: Share of {{simCount}} independent Monte Carlo scenarios that reach the balance target, simulating a random daily P&L from μ and σ.

errors.targetTooLow: Target must be higher than current balance.
errors.negativeMu: Negative average daily gain — projection not possible.
errors.insufficientData: Not enough trading days for a reliable projection (minimum 5).

horizon.failure: {{failureRate}}% of simulations do not reach {{target}} within the {{maxDays}} simulated trading-day horizon.

milestone.next: {{balance}} in {{days}} ({{weeks}}) — about {{pct}}% of the way to your goal.

typical.withDuration: Median time {{days}} (about {{weeks}}) — central scenario between fast and prudent.
typical.insufficient: Median timeline is not available with the current data.

assumptions.adjusted: μ and σ adjusted for your target size (×{{ratio}}).

history.standard: {{dayCount}} trading days included from the full account history.
history.limited: Only {{dayCount}} trading days (minimum {{minDays}} to project); interpret with caution.

probability.certainty: {{probability}}% chance of reaching {{target}} — near statistical certainty with your current profile.
probability.veryLikely: {{probability}}% chance of reaching {{target}} — the target is very likely with your current profile.
probability.likely: {{probability}}% chance of reaching {{target}} — the target is realistic with your current profile.
probability.uncertain: {{probability}}% chance of reaching {{target}} — the outcome remains uncertain despite a positive edge.
probability.unlikely: {{probability}}% chance of reaching {{target}} — the target looks difficult with your current profile.
probability.veryUnlikely: {{probability}}% chance of reaching {{target}} — the target is unlikely within the simulated horizon.
probability.withSizing: Projection based on your target size (μ and σ scaled ×{{ratio}}).

timeline.range: The realistic range is {{monthsMin}} to {{monthsMax}} months of trading depending on your consistency.
timeline.withDateRange: The realistic range is {{monthsMin}} to {{monthsMax}} months of trading depending on your consistency, around {{dateRange}}.
timeline.singleMonth: The realistic timeline is about {{months}} months of trading depending on your consistency.
timeline.singleMonthWithDateRange: The realistic timeline is about {{months}} months of trading, around {{dateRange}}.
timeline.insufficient: The estimated timeline is not available with the current data.
timeline.relaxed: No need to rush or force trades to get there faster.
timeline.patient: Variability between optimistic and prudent scenarios is notable — keep your pace without forcing entries.
timeline.cautious: Probability remains modest: prioritize execution quality rather than accelerating your pace.
timeline.steady: Stay consistent in your trading routine to converge toward this range.

sizing.sectionTeaser: e.g. 1 MNQ lot at $2/point — click here to scale μ and σ to your target size
sizing.sectionActive: Adjustment active — edit lots and $/point below
sizing.assumptionNote: Linear adjustment: assumes the same edge per $/point as your history.

errors.insufficientExposureData: Not enough trades with known exposure to adjust size (minimum 5).

postLossSizing.title: Size after a loss
postLossSizing.subtitle: After each losing trade: was the next trade's exposure (contracts × point value) larger, equal, or smaller? Micro and e-mini in the same family (e.g. NQ / MNQ) are compared fairly.
postLossSizing.noData: Not enough loss-then-next-trade cases for the selected period.
postLossSizing.sampleSizeTooltip: Number of losses followed immediately by another trade, within the period chosen at the top of the page (account, dates, strategy). Not a rolling window: each loss counts at most once in that range.
postLossSizing.vsLosingTradeTooltip: Next trade exposure (contracts × point value) vs the losing trade, same underlying (micro and e-mini grouped).
postLossSizing.vsMedianTooltip: Next trade exposure vs the median of your last {{count}} trades in the same contract family on the account.
postLossSizing.skippedCrossInstrument: {{count}} case(s) skipped: different instrument after the loss.
postLossSizing.largerPctKpiTooltip: Share of post-loss trades where size is greater than the losing trade.
postLossSizing.avgPnlAfterLargerTooltip: When you increase size after a loss. Positive: the next trade makes you money on average. Negative: the next trade costs you money on average — typical revenge trading.
postLossSizing.avgPnlTooltip: Average P/L of the trade after a loss (this size category). Positive: profitable on average. Negative: losing on average.
postLossSizing.recommendation: Take a break after a loss. Wait at least 30 minutes before the next trade.

interpretationScale.title: How to read the percentages
interpretationScale.intro: Each share shows how often the next trade falls into that category after a loss. Aim for a low « Larger » share.
interpretationScale.kpiHint: Goal: keep this rate as low as possible.

kpiVerdict.good: Strong discipline: you rarely increase size after a loss.
kpiVerdict.neutral: Watch closely: moderate tendency to size up after a loss.
kpiVerdict.bad: Risky behavior: frequent size increases (likely revenge trading).

interpretationScale.limitedSample: Limited sample ({{count}} cases): interpret with caution.
interpretationScale.largerDesc: Larger size: typical revenge-trading signal and emotional risk-taking.
interpretationScale.equalDesc: Same size: moderate discipline; watch your trade frequency.
interpretationScale.smallerDesc: Smaller size: good risk-management reflex after a loss.

postWinSizing.title: Size after a win
postWinSizing.subtitle: After each winning trade: was the next trade's exposure (contracts × point value) larger, equal, or smaller? Micro and e-mini in the same family (e.g. NQ / MNQ) are compared fairly.
postWinSizing.noData: Not enough win-then-next-trade cases for the selected period.
postWinSizing.sampleSizeTooltip: Number of wins followed immediately by another trade, within the period chosen at the top of the page (account, dates, strategy). Not a rolling window: each win counts at most once in that range.
postWinSizing.vsWinningTradeTooltip: Next trade exposure (contracts × point value) vs the winning trade, same underlying (micro and e-mini grouped).
postWinSizing.vsMedianTooltip: Next trade exposure vs the median of your last {{count}} trades in the same contract family on the account.
postWinSizing.skippedCrossInstrument: {{count}} case(s) skipped: different instrument after the win.
postWinSizing.largerPctKpiTooltip: Share of post-win trades where size is greater than the winning trade.
postWinSizing.avgPnlAfterLargerTooltip: When you increase size after a win. Positive: the next trade makes you money on average. Negative: the next trade costs you money on average — overconfidence or euphoria costing you money.
postWinSizing.avgPnlTooltip: Average P/L of the trade after a win (this size category). Positive: profitable on average. Negative: losing on average.
postWinSizing.recommendation: After a win, keep size aligned with your plan. Avoid increasing size out of euphoria.

interpretationScale.title: How to read the percentages
interpretationScale.intro: Each share shows how often the next trade falls into that category after a win. Aim for a low « Larger » share.
interpretationScale.kpiHint: Goal: keep this rate as low as possible.

kpiVerdict.good: Strong discipline: you rarely increase size after a win.
kpiVerdict.neutral: Watch closely: moderate tendency to size up after a win.
kpiVerdict.bad: Risky behavior: frequent size increases (likely overconfidence).

interpretationScale.limitedSample: Limited sample ({{count}} cases): interpret with caution.
interpretationScale.largerDesc: Larger size: typical overconfidence signal and excessive risk-taking after a winning streak.
interpretationScale.equalDesc: Same size: sound discipline; watch your trade frequency.
interpretationScale.smallerDesc: Smaller size: good risk-management reflex after a win.

multiCurrency.noConversionWarning: Multiple active currencies without FX conversion: aggregated amounts are hidden. Select a single account or check your default currency in Settings.
multiCurrency.partialTabNote: Without FX conversion: percentages, counts, and size distributions remain visible; only aggregated amounts (average P/L) are hidden.

intro.heading: Overview
intro.tradeCount: {{count}} trades analyzed for the selected period.
intro.excellent: Excellent period: your metrics and discipline confirm a real edge. Congratulations on this level of consistency.
intro.positive: Strong momentum: you have solid foundations on this period. Keep building on what works.
intro.mixed: Mixed period: strengths to capitalize on and a few habits to adjust. Staying aware keeps you on track.
intro.challenging: A demanding period — refocus on discipline and position size. Progress often starts there.

insufficientData.title: Summary
insufficientData.body: Not enough trades in the selected period (minimum {{min}}) to generate a behavioral summary.
insufficientData.partial: Available data is not yet sufficient for a detailed summary on this period.

title.positive: What is working well for you

strengths.profitFactorGood: Your profit factor of {{profitFactor}} is excellent — each dollar lost earns almost {{profitFactor}} dollars in gross gains.
strengths.profitFactorAverage: Your profit factor of {{profitFactor}} shows a positive edge: consolidate what works and cut the costliest setups.
strengths.profitFactorPoor: Your profit factor of {{profitFactor}} is tight on this period — refocus on execution quality and size before volume.
strengths.sharpeGood: Your annualized Sharpe of {{sharpe}} is good — solid return for the risk taken.
strengths.sharpeExcellent: Your annualized Sharpe of {{sharpe}} is exceptional: well done on this consistency.
strengths.sharpeAverage: Your annualized Sharpe of {{sharpe}} is fair — smoothing volatility can still improve consistency.
strengths.sharpePoor: Your annualized Sharpe of {{sharpe}} reflects an unstable period — prioritize steady risk trade by trade.
strengths.winRateGood: Your {{winRate}} % win rate is solid on this period.
strengths.winRateAverage: Your {{winRate}} % win rate is in a middle zone — execution quality can make the difference.
strengths.winRatePoor: Your {{winRate}} % win rate is low — prioritize selectivity and plan adherence.
strengths.expectancyPositive: Your per-trade expectancy is positive ({{expectancy}}): you earn on average per trade.
strengths.expectancyNegative: Your per-trade expectancy is negative ({{expectancy}}) on this period — tighten risk.
strengths.streaks: Your maximum winning streak of {{maxWins}} trades in a row, with only {{maxLosses}} losers in a row, shows real consistency.
strengths.encourageNoMetrics: Overall numbers are demanding on this period, but each session is a fresh start: anchor on your plan and position size.

risk.drawdownHigh: Max drawdown {{drawdownPct}} % ({{drawdownAmount}}) — protect capital before increasing size.
risk.drawdownModerate: Max drawdown {{drawdownPct}} % ({{drawdownAmount}}): manageable if you stick to your plan.
risk.recoveryGood: Recovery factor {{recovery}}: you bounce back efficiently after dips.
risk.recoveryLow: Recovery factor {{recovery}}: losses take longer to earn back.

rhythm.overtrading: About {{count}} trades per active day on average — beyond 10, decision quality often drops.
rhythm.healthyPace: Healthy pace: about {{count}} trades per active day (3–5 is ideal for day trading).
rhythm.worstDayNoMoney: Toughest day observed: {{day}} (amounts hidden — multiple currencies).

postLoss.larger: After a loss you often size up — see the « {{tab}} » tab for detail.
postLoss.equal: After a loss you mostly keep the same size — good habit (see « {{tab}} »).
postLoss.smaller: After a loss you often size down — prudent discipline (see « {{tab}} »).
postLoss.insufficient: Not enough post-loss cases to summarize sizing habit.

postWin.larger: After a win you often size up — watch overconfidence (tab « {{tab}} »).
postWin.equal: After a win you keep size — good discipline (tab « {{tab}} »).
postWin.smaller: After a win you often size down (tab « {{tab}} »).

process.planRespectGood: On {{comparableCount}} comparable winning trades (planned and actual R/R), {{rate}} % achieved actual R/R greater than or equal to planned R/R.
process.planRespectLow: On {{comparableCount}} comparable winning trades (planned and actual R/R), only {{rate}} % met planned R/R — often an early exit or a wider stop than planned.
process.rrGap: Planned R/R {{planned}} vs realized {{actual}} on average (realized on winners).

title.cleanCelebrate: Discipline: you are holding the line

alerts.revengeEncourage: After a losing day, you average {{afterWin}} to {{afterLoss}} trades the next day — a habit to watch to protect your capital.
alerts.revengeWorstMonth: This may partly explain losses concentrated in {{month}}.
alerts.sizingEncourage: Your losing positions are on average {{pct}} % larger than winners — try to keep the same conviction regardless of expected outcome.
alerts.congratulateClean: Well done: no major revenge trading or inverted sizing signals on this period. Your discipline is holding up.
alerts.neutralClean: No major frequency or sizing alerts — stay mindful on tougher days.
alerts.none: No major frequency or sizing discipline alerts on this period.

timeWindows.bestHours: You perform clearly better around {{hours}} ({{timezone}} timezone).
timeWindows.bestHoursCelebrate: Well done: your best results cluster around {{hours}} ({{timezone}} timezone).
timeWindows.bestAndWorstHoursCelebrate: Well done: your best results cluster around {{hours}}, while the {{hour}} slot is your least productive ({{timezone}} timezone).
timeWindows.worstHour: Your weakest performance is on the {{hour}} slot ({{timezone}} timezone).
timeWindows.worstHourCelebrate: Conversely, the {{hour}} slot is your least productive ({{timezone}} timezone).
timeWindows.worstHourEncourage: The {{hour}} slot is weaker — you perform better elsewhere; worth avoiding for now ({{timezone}} timezone).
timeWindows.worstHourNoMoney: Your weakest performance is on the {{hour}} slot ({{timezone}} timezone).
timeWindows.weekdays: {{bestDay}} is your best day ({{bestWinRate}} % win rate, {{bestPnl}}), {{worstDay}} the trickiest.
timeWindows.weekdaysCelebrate: Congratulations: {{bestDay}} is your strongest day ({{bestWinRate}} % win rate, {{bestPnl}}), while {{worstDay}} needs more caution.
timeWindows.weekdaysNoMoney: {{bestDay}} is your best day ({{bestWinRate}} % win rate), {{worstDay}} the trickiest — aggregated amounts hidden (multiple currencies).

duration.bestAvg: Your best average P&L is on the {{label}} bucket ({{avgPnl}}).
duration.bestAvgLowVolume: Your best average P&L is on {{label}} ({{avgPnl}}), with only {{count}} trades — confirm on a larger sample.
duration.sweetSpot: Your sweet spot is {{label}} — good win rate ({{winRate}} %) and return ({{avgPnl}}, {{count}} trades).
duration.sweetSpotCelebrate: Well done: the {{label}} bucket is your ideal zone ({{winRate}} % win rate, {{avgPnl}} on average, {{count}} trades).
duration.onlyBucket: The {{label}} bucket offers your best balance ({{winRate}} % win rate, {{avgPnl}} average P&L).

trajectory.bestWeek: Your week {{week}} ({{year}}) stands out as the best ({{pnl}}, {{winRate}} % win rate).
trajectory.bestWeekCelebrate: Congratulations: week {{week}} ({{year}}) is your performance peak ({{pnl}}, {{winRate}} % win rate).
trajectory.bestWeekNoMoney: Best week by win rate: W{{week}} {{year}} ({{winRate}} %).
trajectory.progression: Overall, you are progressing in the second half of the period — keep going in that direction.
trajectory.progressionCelebrate: Nice progress in the second half of the period: your curve is heading the right way.
trajectory.volatile: The trajectory remains mixed — spot tougher weeks to anticipate them better.
trajectory.volatileEncourage: The period was volatile — that is normal. Refocus on your process: consistency pays off over time.

behaviorDiscipline.noData: Not enough data in the selected period to compute this indicator.

revenge.title: Discipline signal — revenge trading
revenge.subtitle: Average number of trades on the trading day after a losing vs winning day (days with activity only). Distinct from the « Size after a loss » tab, which analyzes the trade immediately after a loss.
revenge.alert: +{{pct}}% more trades after a losing day — possible revenge trading signal.
revenge.alertFavorable: {{pct}}% fewer trades after a losing day — good frequency discipline.

sizing.title: Position size — sizing discipline
sizing.subtitle: Average exposure (contracts × point value) on winning vs losing trades. Micro and e-mini in the same family are compared fairly.
sizing.alert: +{{pct}}% larger size on losing trades than winners — risky overconviction bias.
sizing.alertFavorable: {{pct}}% smaller size on losing trades — sound sizing discipline.

alerts.title: Behavioral alert signals
alerts.revengeSummary: Revenge trading: on average {{afterLoss}} trades/day after a losing day vs {{afterWin}} after a winning day (+{{pct}}%), which can amplify large negative days.
alerts.sizingSummary: Inverted sizing: losing positions are on average {{pct}}% larger than winners — tendency to overcommit on setups that turn out wrong.

dashboard.title: Trade Statistical Analysis
dashboard.subtitle: Discover your best setups, identify losing patterns and detect behavioral biases

bestSetups.title: Best Setups
bestSetups.noData: Not enough data (minimum 30 trades per setup)

descriptions.overtrading: Average of {{count}} trades on active days
descriptions.fomo: {{count}} trades with FOMO signs (late entries, better entry missed)
descriptions.lossAversion: {{count}} losing trades held too long or with widened SL

recommendations.limitTrades: Limit the number of trades per day
recommendations.takePauseAfterLoss: Take a break after a loss. Wait at least 30 minutes before the next trade.
recommendations.waitForSetup: Wait for the right setup. Don't chase the market.
recommendations.cutLossesQuickly: Respect the initial stop loss. Cut losses quickly.
recommendations.letWinnersRun: Let winners run. Respect take profit targets.
recommendations.neverWidenSL: Respect the initial stop loss. Never widen the SL.

biasRules.title: Detection Rules & Configuration
biasRules.helpText: Customize detection thresholds according to your trading style
biasRules.resetConfirm: Reset all thresholds to default values?

overtrading.title: Overtrading
overtrading.explanation: Optimal day trading is between 3-5 trades per day. Beyond 10 trades often indicates overtrading which can reduce decision quality.
overtrading.minDaysDesc: Number of days with overtrading to confirm the pattern

revengeTrading.title: Revenge Trading
revengeTrading.explanation: Trading for revenge after a loss is one of the most destructive behaviors. Take a break after a loss to avoid emotional decisions.
revengeTrading.minOccurrencesDesc: Minimum number to detect the pattern

fomo.title: FOMO (Fear Of Missing Out)
fomo.explanation: Entering late into a move for fear of missing an opportunity often leads to entries at the worst moment. Patience and discipline are essential.
fomo.minOccurrencesDesc: Minimum number to detect the pattern

lossAversion.title: Loss Aversion
lossAversion.explanation: Holding losing positions too long hoping for a reversal is a classic bias. Respecting your stop loss is crucial.
lossAversion.minOccurrencesDesc: Minimum number to detect the pattern

prematureExit.title: Premature Exit
prematureExit.explanation: Cutting winners too early for fear of losing gains prevents reaching profit targets. Letting winners run is essential.
prematureExit.minOccurrencesDesc: Minimum number to detect the pattern
prematureExit.rrThresholdDesc: Ratio of planned R:R considered premature (0.5 = 50%)

stopLossWidening.title: Stop Loss Widening
stopLossWidening.explanation: Widening your stop loss after entry is one of the most dangerous behaviors. The initial stop loss must always be respected.
stopLossWidening.minOccurrencesDesc: Minimum number to detect the pattern
